metadata.summary: Conversation-first growth for Threads: post 5+ punchy posts daily, reply to large accounts, adapt from Twitter/Instagram. No links; suppress promotional content. No write API; all posting manual.

content.title: Threads Growth
content.summary: A conversation-first growth system for Threads: post 5+ short punchy posts per day, reply thoughtfully to large accounts to drive discovery, and adapt content from Instagram or Twitter to Threads' casual tone. No links in posts — Threads suppresses promotional content. No official third-party posting API as of 2026; all posting is manual.

why.p: Threads rewards authenticity signals — genuine disagreement, admitted uncertainty, and follow-up questions outperform polished takes. One early, thoughtful reply on a 100K-follower post outperforms five standalone posts for discovery.

The platform is tied to Instagram: cross-promoting from Instagram Stories ("join the conversation on Threads") is the fastest follower import mechanism, and any Instagram account restriction affects Threads access.

list.item: Generating daily post batches (20-30 drafts) across hot takes, this-or-that, fill-in-blank, experience stories, and predictions
Drafting reply templates for a specific niche (to be edited and sent by a human)
Planning a cross-platform content flow from Instagram or Twitter to Threads
Repurposing Twitter/X thread content into Threads-native conversational format
Writing a Month 2-3 optimization plan: which post types to double down on
Automated posting or replying — no official API for write access; all posting is manual
Engagement pod coordination — Threads community strongly penalizes inauthentic engagement
Generating long-form content — Threads punishes long posts; max 300 characters is target
Accounts without an Instagram presence — Threads account depends on Instagram; fix that first
Generating posts that mimic trends without knowing current platform culture

rule.statement: Reply to large accounts first — one early, thoughtful reply on a 100K-follower post outperforms five standalone posts for discovery.
rule.rationale: Threads surfaces engaged replies to the original post's audience. Early replies get the most exposure; late replies are buried.

rule.statement: Use the [Agree/Disagree] + [Add perspective] + [Question] reply formula. Never write "Great point!" as a reply.
rule.rationale: Generic agreement adds no value and signals low-quality engagement. Questions invite the original poster to respond, creating a visible conversation thread.

example.note: Reply to a post about shipping fast: "This is so true. I found the same thing when building [X] — the constraint of a hard deadline produced better decisions than open-ended sprints. What made you realize this?"
Reply: "Great point! So insightful. Totally agree." — No perspective added, no question, no reason for the original poster or their audience to engage further.

rule.statement: Post 5+ short punchy posts per day across these types: hot takes, this-or-that, fill-in-blank, experience prompts, and predictions.
rule.rationale: Threads rewards high volume of conversational posts. Each post type generates a different engagement pattern — questions drive replies, hot takes drive quote posts.

list.item: Hot take: "Unpopular opinion: [bold statement]. Change my mind."
This or that: "[A] or [B]?" (e.g., "Notion or Obsidian?")
Fill in blank: "The best [X] is ___"
Experience: "Raise your hand if you've ever [relatable struggle]"
Prediction: "In [year], [specific prediction about your niche]"

rule.statement: Do not include links in Threads posts. Threads does not render links and promotional posts are heavily suppressed.
rule.rationale: Threads's algorithm suppresses posts that look promotional or are designed to drive traffic off-platform. Use the bio link for all external CTAs.

rule.statement: Cross-promote from Instagram Stories by sharing a specific Threads post with "join the conversation on Threads" — this is the fastest follower import mechanism.
rule.rationale: Instagram followers already trust the account. Directing them to Threads via Stories converts at a much higher rate than organic Threads discovery.

rule.statement: When adapting Twitter/X content for Threads: shorten to 1-3 posts, remove thread numbering, make it conversational, add a genuine question at the end, remove promotional language.
rule.rationale: Twitter content is declarative and formal. Threads culture rewards rough edges, admitted uncertainty, and genuine curiosity. Verbatim cross-posts underperform.

rule.statement: Track follower growth rate (target 5-10% weekly), replies per post, and profile visits per day as primary Threads KPIs.
rule.rationale: Threads provides almost no exportable analytics. Manual tracking in a spreadsheet is required. These three metrics are observable without API access.

list.item: Followers: starting = building, 100+/week = growing, 500+/week = established
Post views: 100 = starting, 1K = growing, 10K+ = established
Replies per post: 2-5 = starting, 10-20 = growing, 50+ = established
Profile visits: 10/day = starting, 50/day = growing, 200+/day = established

antipattern.description: Just cross-posting content from Twitter without adaptation.
antipattern.reason: Twitter content is declarative and polished. Threads culture rewards casual, curious, rough-edged posts. Verbatim cross-posts feel out of place and underperform.

antipattern.description: Being too promotional — product pitches in posts.
antipattern.reason: Threads's algorithm heavily penalizes promotional content. The platform norms are 95% value, 5% promo. Any promotional feel reduces distribution.

antipattern.description: Posting once a day instead of 5+ posts daily.
antipattern.reason: Threads requires higher post volume than LinkedIn or Twitter because the feed is highly active and content decays quickly. One daily post is not enough for the algorithm.

antipattern.description: Using a formal or corporate tone.
antipattern.reason: Threads culture is conversational and casual. Corporate language signals inauthenticity and the audience does not engage.

rule.statement: Batch-generate 35+ post drafts per week but post only what feels genuinely on-voice. Volume generation is useful; volume posting without editing is harmful.
rule.rationale: Agent output defaults to polished and authoritative. Threads requires rough edges and genuine curiosity. Always rewrite agent output before posting.

rule.statement: As of 2026, Threads has no official write API. All posting must be done manually by a human. Agent output is drafts only.
rule.rationale: Meta's Threads API (2024) is read-only. Any tool claiming to post to Threads on your behalf is violating ToS and risks account suspension.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use the agent as a content ideation and adaptation engine, not an execution tool. Given the week's content pillars and ICP, produce 20-30 post draft options across all conversation-starter types. The human selects, edits for personal voice, and posts manually. For cross-platform adaptation, feed the agent the original Twitter or Instagram caption and ask it to rewrite in Threads tone (conversational, lower-stakes, question-ending). No agent subagents are needed for posting; all execution is human.

p.term: faion-sdd-executor-agent — for planning and tracking the Month 2-3 Threads growth SDD tasks

section.name: prompt-pattern
section.code:
You are a Threads content strategist for <niche>. ICP: <icp>. Tone: casual, direct, curiosity-driven.
Generate 20 Threads post drafts. Distribute across: - 5 hot takes ("Unpopular opinion: ...") - 5 this-or-that questions - 5 fill-in-the-blank prompts - 3 personal experience stories (2-3 sentences) - 2 predictions for <year>
Each post: max 300 characters, no hashtags, end with an implicit or explicit invitation to respond.

Adapt this Twitter/X thread for Threads: <twitter_thread>
Rules: - Shorten to 1-3 posts max - Remove thread numbering - Make it conversational, not declarative - Add a genuine question at the end - Remove any promotional language

p.term: Meta Graph API (Threads) — Read public Threads posts and basic profile data (https://developers.facebook.com/docs/threads); requests (Python) — Call Threads API endpoints (pip install requests); Buffer — Schedule Threads posts (added Threads support 2024) (https://buffer.com/threads)

Threads (Meta, SaaS, partial agent-friendly) — Official API is limited; write access via Meta partnership only; Buffer (SaaS, agent-friendly API) — Added Threads scheduling in 2024; basic analytics; Metricool (SaaS, agent-friendly API) — Threads analytics + scheduling where API permits; Later (SaaS, partial) — Limited Threads support; check current API status; Meta Business Suite (SaaS, agent-friendly Graph API) — Threads insights at account level

list.item: Post volume on Threads (5+ posts/day) is higher than any other text platform; batch-generate 35+ posts per week but only post what feels genuinely on-voice
Replies to large accounts drive disproportionate discovery — one early, thoughtful reply on a 100K-follower post outperforms 5 standalone posts
Threads rewards authenticity signals: genuine disagreement, admitted uncertainty, and follow-up questions outperform polished takes
Do not include links in posts — Threads does not render links and promotional posts are heavily suppressed
Cross-promote from Instagram Stories by sharing a specific Threads post with "join the conversation on Threads" — this is the fastest follower import mechanism
Build reply conversations by replying to your own posts with follow-up thoughts; algorithmic signals treat thread-style self-replies as active conversations

p.term: Agent defaults to polished, authoritative voice; Threads culture rewards rough edges and genuine curiosity — always rewrite agent output before posting
Agent cannot schedule or post to Threads autonomously; all output is drafts for human posting
Threads provides almost no exportable analytics data; growth measurement requires manual tracking in a spreadsheet
Threads features and algorithms were still evolving rapidly as of early 2026; agent knowledge of "best practices" should be treated as provisional
Threads account is tied to Instagram; any Instagram account issue (ban, restriction) affects Threads access — do not treat them as independent

template.purpose: Agent prompt: 20 Threads post drafts across all conversation-starter types
Strip tweet numbering, split into ≤3 Threads-native posts, append question
